INGREDIENTS:
<ul>
<li>water (4 liters)</li>
<li>salt (2 tbsp)</li>
<li>guanciale (200 g)</li>
<li>pancetta (200 g)</li>
<li>eggs (3)</li>
<li>egg yolks (2)</li>
<li>pecorino romano (100 g, finely grated)</li>
<li>black pepper (generous amount, freshly ground)</li>
<li>spaghetti (400 g)</li>
<li>pasta water (1 cup)</li>
</ul>

HEADNOTES:


INSTRUCTIONS:
<ol>
<li>Bring a large <span class="cookware">pot</span> of <span class="ingredient" data-amount="4" data-unit="liters">water</span> to boil. Season generously with <span class="ingredient" data-amount="2" data-unit="tbsp">salt</span>.</li>
<li>While waiting, cut <span class="ingredient" data-amount="200" data-unit="g">guanciale</span> into small strips. If unavailable, use <span class="ingredient" data-amount="200" data-unit="g">pancetta</span> instead.</li>
<li>Whisk together <span class="ingredient" data-amount="3" data-unit="">eggs</span>, <span class="ingredient" data-amount="2" data-unit="">egg yolks</span>, and <span class="ingredient" data-amount="100" data-unit="g, finely grated">pecorino romano</span> in a <span class="cookware">mixing</span> bowl. Season with <span class="ingredient" data-amount="generous amount, freshly ground" data-unit="">black pepper</span>.</li>
<li>Cook <span class="ingredient" data-amount="400" data-unit="g">spaghetti</span> in the boiling water for <button type="button" class="timer" data-value="2" data-unit="minutes" aria-label="Start 2 minutes timer">2 minutes</button> less than package directions.</li>
<li>Meanwhile, cook the guanciale in a cold <span class="cookware">skillet</span> over medium heat for <button type="button" class="timer" data-value="8" data-unit="minutes" aria-label="Start 8 minutes timer">8 minutes</button> until fat renders and meat is crispy.</li>
<li>Reserve <span class="ingredient" data-amount="1" data-unit="cup">pasta water</span>, then drain pasta. Off heat, add pasta to skillet, then quickly stir in egg mixture. Toss vigorously, adding pasta water as needed to create a creamy sauce.</li>
<li>Serve immediately with additional pecorino and black pepper.</li>
</ol>
